Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Standard P2P process flow 1 Create Purchase Requisition PR 2 Approve Purchase Requisition PR 3 Issue Purchase Order PO 4 Receive Goods 5 Vendor Invoice Received 6 Match Invoice to PO 3 way match PO Receipt Invoice 7 Release Payment 8 Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets evaluate each case based on this standard flow and logical ordering using timestamps

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 This looks perfectly standard and follows the logical sequence

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 Issue PO happens before Approve PR Vendor Invoice Received then Release Payment then Approve PR then Receive Goods then Match Invoice to PO

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomalies PR approved after PO is issued Payment released before receiving goods Payment released before invoice is matched to PO In fact PR approved after payment is released

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 Release Payment then Vendor Invoice Received then Receive Goods then Match Invoice

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomalies Payment released before invoice received before goods received and before Match Invoice

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 Receive Goods before Issue Purchase Order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomalies Goods received before PO is issued

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Why these are problematic Case 1002 Paying for goods not received and unapproved requests Risk of paying for unauthorized purchases Receiving goods after paying is risky if vendor defaults

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1003 Paying without an invoice or goods receipt means paying blind No verification of receipt or billing accuracy

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1004 Receiving goods without a PO makes it hard to track commitments match invoices properly and manage supplier performance Could lead to unauthorized purchases slipping through
